summary: Phase 9 testing complete: Continuous IDW-interpolated heatmap, cursor inspector, Lab experiments with sensitivity sweep, and AI grounding enhancements all working. Backend: 12/13 tests passed (92.3%). Frontend: All Phase 9 features passed.

minor_issues.issue: LLM API budget exceeded during richer context test
minor_issues.impact: Advisor chat may fail when budget limit is reached
minor_issues.note: This is an API budget limitation, not a code bug. The no-hallucination test passed, confirming strict grounding is working correctly.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=temperature (91 points, unit °C, provenance)
Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=humidity (91 points, unit %)
Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=precipitation (91 points, unit mm (7d))
Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=drought_spi (62 points, unit SPI)
Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=rainfall_departure (33 points, unit % vs LPA)
Backend: GET /api/climate/grid?layer=invalid returns 422
Backend: GET /api/climate/point?lat=22.5&lon=80 (returns temp, humidity, wind, nearest_place)
Backend: POST /api/lab/run with 2 experiments and narrative (18s response time)
Backend: POST /api/lab/run with include_narrative=false (fast mode, no LLM)
Backend: POST /api/lab/sensitivity with warming_c sweep (5 points, 0-4°C range)
Backend: Advisor no-hallucination test (correctly refused out-of-scope Mars question)
Frontend US-24: Continuous IDW-interpolated heatmap (ImageOverlay) covering India
Frontend US-25: Map legend with smooth gradient bar and 'IDW-interpolated · hover for exact value'
Frontend US-26: Cursor inspector tooltip on hover showing coords, value, nearest place
Frontend US-27: Layer switching functionality (map reloads with different data layers)
Frontend US-28: Lab page with experiment cards and 5 variable sliders (warming, rainfall, urbanization, land-use, horizon)
Frontend US-29: Add experiment button (2nd card appeared), Run All button present
Frontend US-30: Sensitivity Sweep tab with all 4 controls (variable, min, max, run)
Frontend US-31: Scientist Lab nav item present in sidebar (data-testid='nav-scientist-lab')
Frontend US-32: Advisor chat UI shows provenance citations (NASA POWER, Open-Meteo ERA5, ECMWF, IMD-style)

phase9_grid_endpoints.details: All grid endpoints working correctly with proper units, provenance, and >50 sample points. Temperature (91 pts), Humidity (91 pts), Precipitation (91 pts, 7-day cumulative), Drought SPI (62 pts), Rainfall Departure (33 pts). Invalid layer correctly returns 422.

phase9_point_query.details: Point query endpoint returns temperature_c, humidity, wind_ms, and nearest_place with correct coordinates.

phase9_lab_experiments.details: Lab run endpoint supports multiple experiments (tested with 2), returns experiments array, summary, provenance, and AI narrative. Fast mode (include_narrative=false) skips LLM as expected.

phase9_sensitivity_sweep.details: Sensitivity endpoint correctly sweeps warming_c from 0-4°C in 5 steps, returns curve with projected_temp_c and projected_rainfall_mm for each point.

phase9_advisor_grounding.details: Advisor strict grounding confirmed via no-hallucination test - correctly refused out-of-scope question about Mars temperature. Richer context test hit LLM API budget limit (not a code bug). UI shows provenance citations prominently.

phase9_heatmap_overlay.details: Continuous IDW-interpolated heatmap renders as ImageOverlay on Leaflet map, covering India with smooth color gradient. No discrete city markers visible, confirming Phase 9 design.

phase9_cursor_inspector.details: Cursor inspector tooltip (data-testid='cursor-inspector') appears on map hover, showing coordinates (22.650°N · 82.457°E), interpolated value (30.1°C), and nearest place (Chhattisgarh).

phase9_map_legend.details: Map legend displays smooth gradient bar with unit and text 'IDW-interpolated · hover for exact value' as specified.

phase9_scientist_lab.details: Scientist Lab page fully functional with experiment cards, 5 variable sliders per experiment, Add Experiment button (max 8), Run All button, and Sensitivity Sweep tab with variable selector, min/max inputs, and Run Sweep button.

performance_notes: Grid endpoints respond in 1-2 seconds (cached after first call)
Lab run with narrative takes ~18 seconds (LLM generation)
Lab run without narrative is fast (<1 second)
Heatmap renders smoothly with 91 sample points
